en: A MASKIL OF DAVID. Blessed is the one whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered.
zh: 過犯得蒙赦免， 罪惡得到遮蓋的人，是有福的。

en: Blessed is the man against whom the LORD counts no iniquity, and in whose spirit there is no deceit.
zh: 心裡沒有詭詐， 耶和華不算為有罪的，這人是有福的。

en: For when I kept silent, my bones wasted away through my groaning all day long.
zh: 我閉口不認罪的時候， 就整天唉哼，以致骨頭衰殘。

en: For day and night your hand was heavy upon me; my strength was dried up as by the heat of summer. Selah
zh: 因為你的手晝夜重壓在我身上， 我的精力耗盡，好像盛暑的乾旱。

en: I acknowledged my sin to you, and I did not cover my iniquity; I said, "I will confess my transgressions to the LORD," and you forgave the iniquity of my sin. Selah
zh: 我向你承認我的罪， 沒有隱藏我的罪孽； 我說：“我要向耶和華承認我的過犯”； 你就赦免我的罪孽。

en: Therefore let everyone who is godly offer prayer to you at a time when you may be found; surely in the rush of great waters, they shall not reach him.
zh: 因此，凡是敬虔的人，都當趁你可尋找的時候，向你禱告； 大水氾濫的時候，必不能達到他那裡。

en: You are a hiding place for me; you preserve me from trouble; you surround me with shouts of deliverance. Selah
zh: 你是我藏身之處， 你必保護我脫離患難， 以得救的歡呼四面環繞我。

en: I will instruct you and teach you in the way you should go; I will counsel you with my eye upon you.
zh: 我要教導你，指示你應走的路； 我要勸戒你，我的眼睛看顧你。

en: Be not like a horse or a mule, without understanding, which must be curbed with bit and bridle, or it will not stay near you.
zh: 你不可像無知的騾馬， 如果不用嚼環轡頭勒住牠們， 牠們就不肯走近。

en: Many are the sorrows of the wicked, but steadfast love surrounds the one who trusts in the LORD.
zh: 惡人必受許多痛苦； 但倚靠耶和華的，必有慈愛四面環繞他。

en: Be glad in the LORD, and rejoice, O righteous, and shout for joy, all you upright in heart!
zh: 義人哪！你們要靠著耶和華歡喜快樂； 所有心裡正直的人哪！你們都要歡呼。
